danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites with distinct color schemes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player character; collision triggers damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, water, lava (implied by level design conventions)
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage zones; contact causes health loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more detailed sprite than standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring pattern recognition and extended engagement

safety_signals.meaning: Safe traversal surface; landing area for navigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items with distinct visual identity
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary or permanent gameplay advantage; health/ability restoration

safety_signals.visual_form: Background areas or clearly demarcated non-interactive spaces
safety_signals.meaning: No hazard presence; visual breathing room between challenges

faction_indicators.color_identity: Likely primary sprite color distinct from enemies

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types suggest different enemy roles/types rather than faction variants

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Changing background art, tile sets, and environmental hazard types per level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawn patterns and behavior complexity increase in later levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character sprite may show visual damage indicators or animation state changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement speed or attack capabilities may be visually telegraphed as reduced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temporary visual effect overlay or character sprite transformation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced movement speed, invincibility flashing, or expanded attack range

environmental_storytelling.example: Sequential level aesthetics (industrial → organic → boss chamber) suggest narrative escalation through visual environment change
Visual concentration of spikes, platforms, and enemies increases toward level end, signaling difficulty ramping
Darker or more saturated colors in later levels communicate increased danger implicitly

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Predictable movement patterns; visible animation loop suggests non-alert state
Animation may change or movement pattern becomes erratic; direction-facing sprite rotates toward player
Larger sprite with more complex animation; attack projectiles or melee swings clearly telegraphed through wind-up frames
Sprite transformation or glow effect; attack range visibly expands

notes: James Pond 2: Codename RoboCod is a straightforward platformer with minimal faction narrative. World-state communication relies on classic 16-bit era conventions: color-coded hazards, sprite-based enemy differentiation, and level theming. No complex political systems detected. Manifestation is primarily through sprite animation, color contrast, and environmental tile design.
